title: Helping developers build safer AI experiences for teens
optimized_headline: Developers Create Safer AI Experiences for Teens: What You Need to Know
raw_excerpt: OpenAI releases prompt-based teen safety policies for developers using gpt-oss-safeguard, helping moderate age-specific risks in AI systems.
raw_body: OpenAI releases prompt-based teen safety policies for developers using gpt-oss-safeguard, helping moderate age-specific risks in AI systems.
speedrun: OpenAI has rolled out new prompt-based teen safety policies for developers using its gpt-oss-safeguard. These guidelines aim to help manage risks specific to younger users interacting with AI systems. The initiative highlights the importance of creating safer online environments for teens. This move is significant as it reflects a growing concern for youth safety in digital spaces.
why_it_matters: Developers can now implement clearer safety measures, directly protecting teens from inappropriate content and interactions.
This shift indicates a broader industry trend toward prioritizing user safety, especially for vulnerable populations like teenagers.

lenses.eli12: OpenAI has introduced new rules for developers to keep teenagers safe when they use AI. Think of it like a safety net that catches harmful content before it reaches young users. This is important because it helps ensure that kids can explore technology without facing risks.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, these new safety policies address a critical user need: the protection of younger audiences. By implementing these guidelines, developers could enhance user trust and engagement. It also emphasizes the importance of building products that prioritize safety and compliance.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, the gpt-oss-safeguard now includes specific prompt-based policies to filter and moderate content for teenage users. This could improve the overall safety of AI interactions by reducing exposure to harmful material. Developers will need to integrate these guidelines effectively to ensure robust protection.
